events.title: K2/Vault-Neo Inversion
events.body: K2 was incorrectly treated as authoritative, causing Karma to stop functioning when K2 went down. This architectural inversion prevented reliable operation.

events.title: Baseline Capabilities Reframe
events.body: Shifted focus from incremental features to closing 7 baseline capabilities first. This prioritizes foundational tools before self-improvement optimization.

events.title: Browser Automation Fix
events.body: Playwright MCP was wired into hub-bridge as a Karma tool. This confirmed Karma's ability to drive browsers, closing a critical capability gap.

events.title: Phase 1 Prioritization
events.body: All baseline gaps (7 capabilities) become Phase 1 requirements. This ensures foundational tools are implemented before advancing to Vesper list features.

events.title: Capability Registry Missing
events.body: Karma forgot previously used tools like chromium. This indicated a critical gap in tracking available capabilities, affecting reliability.

events.title: Capability Registry Added
events.body: Spine now stores both behavioral patterns and available capabilities. This resolved Karma's memory loss for tools and ensured consistent functionality.

events.title: Session Chunking Strategy
events.body: Long sessions will be split into 20-turn windows for thorough review. This enables comprehensive analysis without context window limitations.
